label: Model merging & weight-space composition

node.label: Model merging & weight-space composition
node.definition: Model merging combines compatible checkpoints or parameter deltas in weight space to obtain a new model without conventional joint retraining.
node.why_it_matters: It offers a low-cost route to combine specialized capabilities, ensembling effects, or independently fine-tuned variants.
limitations: Parameter alignment, interference, tokenizer mismatch, and evaluation across merged capabilities remain difficult.

dependencies.rationale: Model merging & weight-space composition uses Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model merging & weight-space composition uses Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model merging & weight-space composition uses Linear algebra as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model merging & weight-space composition uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.

Parameter alignment, interference, tokenizer mismatch, and evaluation across merged capabilities remain difficult.
